content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

May 30, 1989

Honorable Tim Leslie
Assemblyman, Fifth District
State Capitol
Sacramento, CA  95814

Re:  Your Request for Advice
     Our File No. A-89-255

Dear Assemblyman Leslie:

     You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided
to you concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform
Act.1/

     Your letter dated April 19, 1989, asks about reimbursement of
expenses which occurred in 1988. You have indicated that the
expenses were appropriate officeholder expenses, and have
summarized my advice that you may be reimbursed for these expenses
out of any of your campaign accounts. This summary is accurate
except that the reimbursement should be made from either your
"restricted funds" (funds received prior to January 1, 1989) or
from funds received on or after January 1, 1989, which have been
designated for your current office or for reelection to your
current office. For your convenience, a copy of your letter is
enclosed.

     Please note that with regard to expenses incurred in 1989,
the Commission will consider at its July meeting adoption of a
proposed regulation which will allow officeholders to be
reimbursed for certain expenses (proposed regulation 18526.1, copy
enclosed). In addition, at its May 2 meeting, the Commission
adopted proposed regulation 18525 (copy enclosed) to assist
incumbent candidates in determining the types of expenses which
must be made from a particular campaign bank account. We
anticipate that regulation 18525 will become effective in August.

___________________________
1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Honorable Tim Leslie
Page 2


Please do not hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5662 if you
have additional questions.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: Carla Wardlow
Political Reform Consultant

Enclosures

STATE CAPITOL
P.O. BOX 942849
SACRAMENTO, CA 94249-0001
(916) 445-4445

COMMITTEES:
EDUCATION
ELECTIONS
PUBLIC EMPLOYEES,
RETIREMENT AND SOCIAL
SECURITY
ASSEMBLY HIGH TECHNOLOGY
CAUCUS
LEGISLATIVE TOURISM CAUCUS
SELECT COMMITTEE ON
ON THE CHANGING FAMILY
SUBCOMMITTEE ON
EDUCATION REFORM

DISTRICT OFFICE
1098 MELODY LANE, SUITE 301
ROSEVILLE, CA 95678
(916) 969-3660
(916) 624-0100

Assembly
California Legislature

TIM LESLIE
ASSEMBLYMAN, FIFTH DISTRICT

April 19, 1989

Ms. Carla Wardlow
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Carla:

     Thank you for looking into my request regarding reimbursement
of expenses incurred by me prior to December 31, 1988 from
personal funds, which were appropriate officeholder expenses.

     I have not been reimbursed for these expenses because of our
desire to comply precisely with FPPC rules and regulations. We
were previously told that regulations were still being prepared
to allow for this situation.

     In our conversation this week you indicated that expenses
incurred by me in 1988 (and determined by me to be officeholder
expenses) for a trip to Washington, D.C. to meet with the
President as well as my local Congressman and expenses for the
purpose of purchasing staff Christmas gifts, can be reimbursed at
this time out of any of my campaign accounts.

     If this understanding is correct, please notify me in writing
as soon as possible.

     Thank you for your prompt assistance to this important
matter.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        TIM LESLIE
                                        Assemblyman, Fifth District

TL:jjk

cc:  Assemblyman Ross Johnson

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

May 2, 1989


Honorable Tim Leslie
Assemblyman, Fifth District
State Capitol
P.O. Box 924849
Sacramento, CA  94249-0001

Dear Assemblyman Leslie:

     We received your letter requesting confirmation of advice
under the Political Reform Act on April 27, 1989.  Your letter has
been assigned to our Technical Assistance and Analysis Division
for response. If you have any questions, you may contact that
division directly at (916) 322-5662.

     If the letter is appropriate for confirmation without further
analysis, we will attempt to expedite our response. A confirming
response will be released after it has gone through our approval
process. If the letter is not appropriate for this treatment, the
staff person assigned to prepare the response will contact you
shortly to advise you. In such cases, the normal analysis, review
and approval process will be followed.

     You should be aware that your letter and our response are
public records which may be disclosed to any interested person
upon receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

                                   Sincerely,

                                   [signature]

                                   Kathryn E. Donovan
                                   General Counsel

KED:plh:confadv1


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

sections.question: Can expenses incurred in 1988 (a trip to Washington, D.C. to meet with the President and local Congressman, and staff Christmas gifts), determined to be appropriate officeholder expenses, be reimbursed from campaign accounts?
sections.conclusion: This summary is accurate except that the reimbursement should be made from either your "restricted funds" (funds received prior to January 1, 1989) or from funds received on or after January 1, 1989, which have been designated for your current office or for reelection to your current office.

sections.question_synthetic: Assemblyman Leslie seeks confirmation that 1988 officeholder expenses paid from personal funds (a Washington D.C. trip and staff Christmas gifts) may be reimbursed from campaign accounts.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC confirms that 1988 officeholder expenses may be reimbursed, but only from restricted funds (pre-January 1, 1989) or funds designated for current office or reelection, not from any campaign account as previously advised.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Multi-part document containing incoming request letter (April 19, 1989), FPPC acknowledgment letter (May 2, 1989), and FPPC response letter (May 30, 1989). Primary document is the May 30 FPPC advice letter. OCR quality appears good with minor formatting variations. File number A-89-255.

CONCLUSION: This summary is accurate except that the reimbursement should be made from either your "restricted funds" (funds received prior to January 1, 1989) or from funds received on or after January 1, 1989, which have been designated for your current office or for reelection to your current office.

embedding.summary: The FPPC confirms telephone advice that Assemblyman Leslie may be reimbursed for appropriate 1988 officeholder expenses, with the clarification that reimbursement must come from restricted funds or funds designated for his current office or reelection. The letter also advises of pending regulations regarding 1989 expense reimbursement.
